# launchdarkly_oauth_client (Resource)

Provides a LaunchDarkly OAuth 2.0 client resource.

This resource allows you to register and manage LaunchDarkly OAuth 2.0 clients. OAuth 2.0 clients let you build custom integrations that use LaunchDarkly as an identity provider. This is an account-level resource; your account may register more than one OAuth 2.0 client.

-> **Note:** The client secret is returned by LaunchDarkly only once, when the client is first created. It is stored in Terraform state but cannot be read back from the API afterward, so it is not populated on import. Be sure your state is configured securely before using this resource. To learn more, read [Sensitive data in state](https://www.terraform.io/docs/state/sensitive-data.html).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "launchdarkly_oauth_client" "internal_dashboard" {
  name         = "Internal dashboard"
  redirect_uri = "https://dashboard.example.com/oauth/callback"
  description  = "OAuth 2.0 client for our internal analytics dashboard"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The human-friendly name for your OAuth 2.0 client.
- `redirect_uri` (String) The redirect URI for your OAuth 2.0 client. This should be an absolute URL conforming with the standard HTTPS protocol.

### Optional

- `description` (String) A description of your OAuth 2.0 client.

### Read-Only

- `account_id` (String) The ID of the account the OAuth 2.0 client is registered under.
- `client_id` (String) The OAuth 2.0 client's unique client ID.
- `client_secret` (String, Sensitive) The OAuth 2.0 client secret. LaunchDarkly returns this value only once, upon creation, so it cannot be recovered on a subsequent read or import.
- `creation_date` (Number) The OAuth 2.0 client's creation date represented as a Unix epoch timestamp in milliseconds.
- `id` (String) The OAuth 2.0 client's unique client ID. This is the same value as `client_id`.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# LaunchDarkly OAuth 2.0 clients can be imported using the client's unique client ID
# Note: the client secret is only returned once, on creation, so it is not populated on import.
terraform import launchdarkly_oauth_client.internal_dashboard 562px796b9eb22d01f6e0a8
```
